projects.name: System Design Knowledge Graph
projects.tagline: FastAPI · NetworkX · trafilatura · [FastMCP](https://github.com/jlowin/fastmcp) · Gemini JSON planner · optional LLM Gateway V2
projects.description: An AI-powered evolving knowledge graph for system design learning. Ingest engineering blog URLs, extract concepts and relationships with LLM agents, persist an evolving graph in `data/graph.json`, and generate contextual learning recommendations.
highlights: LLM orchestrator: Gemini chooses MCP tools each turn (extract → concepts → relationships → merge → recommendations)
**Live agent log** in the UI via Server-Sent Events (`POST /ingest/stream`)
Structured JSON agents: Pydantic-validated concept, relationship, and recommendation outputs (`prompts/*.txt`)
Persistent graph: NetworkX merged into `data/graph.json` with Mermaid visualization

diagram.content:
flowchart TB
    subgraph Client
        UI[frontend/index.html]
    end

    subgraph App["FastAPI :8000"]
        API[backend/main.py]
        LOOP[backend/agent_loop.py]
        PLAN[backend/llm_planner.py]
        SSE[backend/agent_orchestrator.py]
        GM[GraphManager]
    end

    subgraph MCP["mcp_server.py stdio"]
        T1[extract_article_tool]
        T2[get_graph_summary]
        T3[extract_concepts]
        T4[extract_relationships]
        T5[merge_graph]
        T6[generate_recommendations]
    end

    subgraph InnerLLM["Inside MCP tools"]
        A1[concept_agent]
        A2[relationship_agent]
        A3[recommendation_agent]
        LLM[backend/utils/llm.py]
    end

    UI -->|POST /ingest/stream| API
    API --> SSE --> LOOP
    LOOP -->|Gemini JSON planner| PLAN
    LOOP -->|fastmcp Client| MCP
    T1 --> T2 --> T3 --> T4 --> T5 --> T6
    T3 & T4 & T6 --> InnerLLM
    InnerLLM --> LLM
    T5 --> GM
    GM --> JSON[(data/graph.json)]
    LOOP --> UI

projects.example:
git clone <your-repo-url>
cd system-design-knowledge-graph
cp .env.example .env
# Edit .env locally only — never commit this file
uv sync --extra dev

projects.name: StockWise
projects.tagline: AI-driven stock recommendations with portfolio tracking, news, and Gemini-powered analysis
projects.description: AI-driven stock recommendation web application with portfolio tracking, market news, technical analysis, and Google Gemini–powered sentiment and explanations.
projects.example:
from utils.llm import llm
from utils.parser import safe_parse_json
from schemas.models import LLMSentimentResponse

raw = await llm.generate_json(prompt, system_instruction=system)
parsed = safe_parse_json(raw, LLMSentimentResponse)

projects.name: Task & Link Manager - Monorepo
projects.tagline: Welcome to the **Task & Link Manager**, a powerful, developer-centric task management system designed to seamlessly brid
projects.description: Welcome to the **Task & Link Manager**, a powerful, developer-centric task management system designed to seamlessly bridge the gap between your browser and a persistent backend service.
highlights: **[Task & Link Manager Extension](task-link-manager-extension)** - A Manifest V3 Chrome extension providing an offline-first task management experience.
**[Task & Link Manager Backend](task-link-manager-backend)** - An Express-based server with SQLite storage and a companion web portal.
**Frontend (Extension)**: Acts as the primary interface. It remains fully functional even when the server is offline, storing all data in the browser's local storage.

**Backend (Server)**: Acts as a centralized data vault. It allows you to backup your tasks and keep multiple browsers in sync. It also features its own web interface for managing tasks without the extension.

diagram.content:
.
├── task-link-manager-backend/    # Node.js + Express server
│   ├── public/                   # Companion Web Portal
│   ├── db.js                     # SQLite database configuration
│   └── server.js                 # API routes & sync logic
└── task-link-manager-extension/  # Chrome Extension (V3)
    ├── popup.js                  # Extension logic & sync engine
    ├── styles.css                # Premium HSL-based design system
    └── index.html                # Extension popup interface

projects.name: PromptForge AI
projects.tagline: Multi-agent pipeline that turns vague ideas into structured, dependency-aware implementation plans
projects.description: PromptForge AI turns vague software ideas into **structured, dependency-aware implementation plans** using a multi-step Gemini pipeline: intent analysis → planning → validation → self-check, with **Pydantic** enforcement at each JSON boundary.

diagram.content:
User (Streamlit UI) 
  → [1. Intent Analysis LLM] 
      ↳ [2. Planner LLM] 
          ↳ [3. Validation LLM] 
              ↳ [4. Self-Check LLM]
                  ↳ [5. Evaluation LLM]
                      → Final Pipeline Result

projects.example: uv run python main.py "Design a support copilot with MCP tools for tickets and order lookup."

projects.name: Job Feed Curator
projects.tagline: LinkedIn feed curator: hide clutter, classify job posts with Gemini + Pydantic
projects.description: Chromium extension that **hides LinkedIn feed clutter** (promoted / ads-style cards) and **de-emphasizes posts that do not look like job leads**, plus a small **Python** service (`uv`) that classifies post snippets with a **Session 5–style qualified prompt** and **Pydantic**-validated JSON.
highlights: This project is intended for **personal productivity** and **coursework**. It **does not** harvest credentials, automate logins, bulk-export LinkedIn data, or impersonate LinkedIn.

You are responsible for complying with **[LinkedIn’s User Agreement](https://www.linkedin.com/legal/user-agreement)** and **[Professional Community Policies](https://www.linkedin.com/legal/professional-community-policies)**. Client-side DOM filtering is a common pattern but **is not a guarantee** of compliance—use at your own risk.
The extension only changes how **your** browser **renders** pages you already opened, plus optional **local** HTTP calls to `127.0.0.1` when **Strict job mode** is enabled.
**Remove** the extension from `chrome://extensions` (or turn off all toggles and reload LinkedIn).

projects.name: Stock News + Price Linking Agent (Chrome Side Panel)
projects.tagline: This project is a Chrome Side Panel extension for multi-step stock research:
projects.description: This project is a Chrome Side Panel extension for multi-step stock research:
highlights: fetch latest company news,
fetch stock prices for the same window,
link news dates with price moves,
return a 1-month buy/not-buy decision,

diagram.content:
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                         Chrome Side Panel                      │
│  UI + Agent Flow (popup_clean.js)                             │
│  - Get Stock From Website                                     │
│  - Run Multi-step Research                                    │
│  - Test Direct Yahoo                                          │
│  - Copy Trace                                                 │
└───────────────┬───────────────────────────────┬───────────────┘
                │                               │
                │                               │
                ▼                               ▼
     ┌───────────────────────┐        ┌──────────────────────────┐
     │ Gemini API            │        │ External Market/News APIs │
     │ (generateContent)     │        │ - Google News RSS         │
     │ model: 2.5-flash-lite │        │ - Yahoo chart/search      │
     └───────────────────────┘        └──────────────────────────┘
                │
                │ (tool orchestration + history)
                ▼
    ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
    │ Direct Data Path in popup_clean.js                        │
    │ get_stock_prices -> Yahoo chart APIs                      │
    │ resolve_ticker   -> NSE/BSE API + Yahoo fallback + fuzzy  │
    └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

projects.example:
python3 -m venv .venv
source .venv/bin/activate
pip install -r requirements.txt

projects.name: Assignment 4: MCP Server with Internet + CRUD + Prefab UI
projects.tagline: A working **Model Context Protocol (MCP) server** built with [FastMCP](https://github.com/jlowin/fastmcp), exposing thre
projects.description: A working **Model Context Protocol (MCP) server** built with [FastMCP](https://github.com/jlowin/fastmcp), exposing three tools — internet fetch, local file CRUD, and a Prefab UI dashboard — to any MCP-compatible client (Claude, Cursor, or a custom script).

diagram.content:
Entry Points
├── fastmcp dev apps server.py   (browser, MCP protocol)
├── python client_demo.py        (stdio transport)
└── Any LLM MCP client           (MCP protocol)
         |
         v
    server.py  (FastMCP server)
    ├── fetch_company_ownership  -->  Wikipedia REST + MediaWiki APIs
    ├── local_file_crud          -->  data/ folder (sandboxed)
    └── ownership_dashboard      -->  data/ folder (read)
                                 -->  Prefab browser preview

projects.example:
cd "assignment_mcp_prefab"
python -m venv .venv
source .venv/bin/activate
pip install -r requirements.txt

projects.name: ResearchPulse
projects.tagline: Chrome extension (Manifest V3) that summarizes **research articles and research-related web pages**, with optional **cit
projects.description: Chrome extension (Manifest V3) that summarizes **research articles and research-related web pages**, with optional **citations** and **follow-up Q&A**, using **Google Gemini**. The API key is **never stored in this repository**—only in your browser after you enter it in the extension settings.
highlights: **No API keys, tokens, or secrets** are hardcoded in the source files.
Keys live only in **`chrome.storage.local`** on each user’s machine after they save settings in the side panel.
Before pushing, avoid committing browser export files, screenshots of settings, or any file that contains your real key.
**Google Chrome** (or another Chromium browser with side panel support).

diagram.content:
manifest.json      # Extension manifest
service-worker.js  # Gemini (see GEMINI_MODEL), shared generateContent helper, arXiv/PMC/citation resolution, summary JSON + citation metadata
content-script.js  # Detects papers / page context on matched sites
sidepanel.html     # Side panel markup (summary type + citations strip)
sidepanel.js       # UI logic, storage, save/resume/history, citation fallbacks
sidepanel.css      # Styles
icons/             # Extension icons (add these locally)
